Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "Hartwell" or "Priscilla" or "delivery" or "empty apartment" (or similar) using search_emails to find Priscilla Holt's complaint email (email_hartwell_delivery_complaint) naming Marcus Webb (Chicago) and Sara Dillon (Houston).
OE 2: Search Airtable with a query related to "Completed" or "Swift" or "verbal" (or similar) using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records on table tblRelocations01 (base appMoveOpsOps001), not scoped to Hartwell Partners only, to find all 4 affected records across Hartwell Partners and Nexagen Bio.
OE 3: Search Linear for Swift-related tickets using linear_list_issues with a query related to "Swift" or "delivery confirmation" or "photo" (or similar), finding OPS-397 (issue_70b4e6271c2f4062beb3231aef495f30, status: done, P2).
OE 4: Read OPS-397's comment history using linear_get_issue or linear_list_comments on issue_70b4e6271c2f4062beb3231aef495f30, OR search Slack #operations (C006) with a query related to "OPS-397" or "photo" or "verbal" or "March 10" or "before" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Noah Reyes's March 17 post warning the fix is prospective only.
OE 5: Search Slack #operations (C006) with a query related to "Nexagen" or "Yuki" or "Reuben" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages to find Chloe Vance's March 18 post identifying Yuki Abe (Austin) and Reuben Torres (Denver) as additionally affected Nexagen Bio employees.
OE 6: Search QuickBooks for pending Swift Logistics vendor bills using quickbooks_search_bills to find BILL-SW-441 ($2,800, Hartwell deliveries, Marcus Webb and Sara Dillon) and BILL-SW-442 ($3,400, Nexagen deliveries, Yuki Abe and Reuben Torres), both Pending.
OE 7: Create a new Linear issue using linear_create_issue tracking the Swift delivery failure incident, with a title referencing "Swift delivery failure" or "unverified deliveries" and a description noting OPS-397's fix does not cover pre-March 10 bookings, 4 records across 2 clients are affected, and bills BILL-SW-441 and BILL-SW-442 should be held.
OE 8: Add a comment to OPS-397 (issue_70b4e6271c2f4062beb3231aef495f30) using linear_create_comment documenting that the photo documentation requirement applies only to bookings from March 10 onward, does not cover the 4 affected moves closed on verbal confirmation, and a new tracking ticket has been created.
OE 9: Update all 4 affected Airtable records using airtable_update_records on table tblRelocations01 for record IDs reccaa8a3a0f5754d, rece7ca6a29d32146, rec6abd296209fc42, rec00ae40cbee0842 (or equivalent such as "Pending Verification").
OE 10: Post a message to #operations (C006) using conversations_add_message summarizing the 4 affected employees across Hartwell Partners and Nexagen Bio, explaining that OPS-397 does not cover pre–March 10 moves and that its photo documentation requirement is prospective (March 10+), confirming that Airtable records have been updated, noting that the 4 deliveries are now disputed
OE 11: Post a message to #vendor-management (channel_id C010) using conversations_add_message, escalating the Swift Logistics SLA breach and summarizing the vendor-level impact: delivery failures, pending bill hold, and the March 22 vendor review context.
OE 12: Reply to Priscilla Holt using reply_to_email (parent: email_hartwell_delivery_complaint) or send_email to priscilla.holt@hartwellpartners.com acknowledging the complaint, naming Marcus Webb and Sara Dillon, and confirming their records have been flagged and the issue is being investigated.
OE 13: Email Finance (Priya Kapoor) using send_email to priya.kapoor@moveops.com requesting that payment be held on bill BILL-SW-441 ($2,800, Hartwell deliveries, Marcus Webb and Sara Dillon) and bill BILL-SW-442 ($3,400, Nexagen deliveries, Yuki Abe and Reuben Torres) until physical delivery is confirmed.
OE 14: Look up Nexagen Bio in the CRM using crm_search_contacts or crm_search_companies to find Daniel Seo (CRM ID: b0f1d7dff49948d69c0fbcf84b2cee6e, email: daniel.seo@nexagenbio.com, HR Coordinator).
OE 15: Send an email using send_email to daniel.seo@nexagenbio.com (Daniel Seo, HR Coordinator, Nexagen Bio) naming Yuki Abe (Austin) and Reuben Torres (Denver) as the affected employees and notifying them that MoveOps is investigating the delivery status.
OE 16: Use calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to with start 2026-03-21 and end 2026-03-25 to find the March 22 'Weekly vendor review with Swift Logistics' event (event ID: d3f7b849c12e4a065d9284f7a630e1c8, 3:00–4:00 PM UTC) on Blessing's calendar.

outcome

outcome

✗Run #4The Finance email was sent to hana.kim@moveops.com, not priya.kapoor@moveops.com. The criterion specifically requires the email to go to Priya Kapoor (priya.kapoor@moveops.com).

7 An email was sent (via `send_email`) to the Nexagen Bio HR contact identified via CRM lookup, proactively notifying them of the potential delivery failure for their employees.
outcome

The agent reported finding no Swift calls and did not update any calendar event. The March 22 vendor review event was not found or updated.
✗Run #6Both calendar read calls (calendar_get_calendar_events_from_to and calendar_search_events) returned empty results — the March 22 Swift vendor review event was not found. No calendar_edit_calendar_event call was made targeting event d3f7b849c12e4a065d9284f7a630e1c8.
9 The comment body (visible in `linear_create_comment` parameters for issue `issue_70b4e6271c2f4062beb3231aef495f30`) states that the photo documentation requirement applies only to bookings from March 10 onward.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✗✓ 83%
✓Run #1The OPS-397 comment states the photo documentation requirement did not cover the March 13–16 moves ('closed out before the photo documentation requirement in this ticket took effect'), names all 4 affected employees with client names and Airtable IDs, satisfying both required elements.
✓Run #2The comment body explicitly states the resolution applied to '"all new bookings" only' and lists all 4 affected employees with their client names, satisfying both required elements.
✓Run #3The `linear_create_comment` body explicitly states 'OPS-397's photo documentation requirement was implemented for *new bookings on or after March 10* only' and names all four affected employees (Marcus Webb, Sara Dillon, Reuben Torres, Yuki Abe) with their client affiliations.
✓Run #4The linear_create_comment body explicitly states the photo requirement 'only applies to bookings made on or after March 10' and lists all 4 affected employees by name with their client companies.
✗Run #5The comment body in linear_create_comment for OPS-397 mentions the 4 affected records and client names, but does NOT state that the photo documentation requirement applies only to bookings from March 10 onward. The comment refers only to 'these March 13–16 moves' without specifying a March 10 cutoff or prospective-only limitation.
✓Run #6The linear_create_comment on OPS-397 (Call 46) documented that the photo requirement only applied to post-March 10 bookings, explaining why OPS-397 being 'done' does not cover the 4 affected moves (naming all affected records/clients).
10 The records array (visible in `airtable_update_records` parameters) includes at least 3 of the 4 correct record IDs: `reccaa8a3a0f5754d` (Marcus Webb), `rece7ca6a29d32146` (Sara Dillon), `rec6abd296209fc42` (Yuki Abe), and `rec00ae40cbee0842` (Reuben Torres).
outcome

query construction

✓Run #4The agent called airtable_list_records on the full Relocations table without any client filter, and also searched by 'Swift' and 'verbal' in the Special Requirements field — neither scoped to Hartwell Partners only.
✓Run #5The agent used airtable_list_records (unfiltered pull of all Relocations records) and airtable_search_records with field 'Special Requirements' and value 'Swift', which are vendor-based and unscoped queries not limited to Hartwell Partners. The broad queries allowed discovery of Nexagen records.
✓Run #6The airtable_search_records call filtered by field 'Status' = 'Completed' (not by client name), and airtable_list_records retrieved all relocations broadly. Neither query was scoped exclusively to Hartwell Partners, allowing all 4 records across both clients to be discovered.
